content.problemStatement:
You're given an array AAA of length NNN, and an integer KKK.

AAA is said to be a nice array if there exists an array BBB of length NNN satisfying the following conditions:


For every index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N), Bi=⌊AiK⌋B_i = \left\lfloor \frac{A_i}{K} \right\rfloorBi​=⌊KAi​​⌋, or Bi=⌈AiK⌉B_i = \left\lceil \frac{A_i}{K} \right\rceilBi​=⌈KAi​​⌉; and
B1+B2+…+BN=0B_1 + B_2 + \ldots + B_N = 0B1​+B2​+…+BN​=0.
Is AAA a nice array?

Note that ⌊  ⌋\left\lfloor \ \ \right\rfloor⌊  ⌋ and ⌈  ⌉\left\lceil \ \ \right\rceil⌈  ⌉ denote the floor and ceiling functions, respectively.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of array AAA and the division parameter.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single string denoting the answer: `"YES"` if AAA is a nice array, and `"NO"` otherwise (without quotes).


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e., the strings `NO`, `No`, `nO`, and `no` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: It's guaranteed that the sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose B=[1,2,−1,2]B = [1, 2, -1, 2]B=[1,2,−1,2], which satisfies Bi=⌊AiK⌋B_i = \left\lfloor \frac{A_i}{K} \right\rfloorBi​=⌊KAi​​⌋ for all iii. The sum of this array is 000, so AAA is nice.
Test case 333: No valid BBB exists, so AAA isn't nice.
Test case 444: B=[−1,1]B = [-1, 1]B=[−1,1] is a valid array, so AAA is nice.
